formal_source_of_truth.closeout_scope: Formalize the promoted baseline as the final comparison source of truth without changing source logic.

final_conclusion.winner_mode_meaning: Derived winner mode records component-level metric winners; it does not assert a scalar overall winner field.
final_conclusion.interpretation: The promoted baseline now stands as a stable component-level comparison surface across both live and repaired replay.

residual.treatment: Carry as a narrow caveat only; do not reopen the promoted baseline without contradictory evidence.

caveats.replay_scope: Current zero-gap evidence is scoped to close-summary and position_closed realized-return fidelity rather than full execution-path equivalence.

dexter_metric_scope_gap.why_it_matters: Path-sensitive derived metrics can still drift even when matched realized-return gap is zero.

caveats.candidate_metric_asymmetry: Mew-X candidate_precision_proxy remains unavailable because creator_candidate is absent under the frozen-source export surface.

frozen_comparative_facts: Live and repaired replay comparisons both remain pass/pass with winner_mode derived.
Stable scored split is Dexter 6, Mew-X 4, tie 2 across live and replay.
Dexter leads candidate generation, signal-to-attempt latency, slippage, realized exit, favorable excursion, and stale-position control.
Mew-X leads fill latency, drawdown containment, time-to-peak, and realized-vs-peak retention.
Dexter and Mew-X replay coverage / gap are both 100% / 0.0000 on the promoted baseline.
Confirmatory residual remains only Mew-X candidate_rejected and does not overturn the promoted baseline.
non_claims: Do not treat derived winner mode as a scalar overall winner.
Do not widen replay parity to full execution-path equivalence.
Do not reopen evidence collection absent contradictory evidence.
recommended_next_cuts: Use the closeout bundle as the frozen baseline for downstream research and execution planning.
If deeper replay claims are needed later, separate path-equivalence work from the current close-summary fidelity claim.
If candidate-surface asymmetry matters downstream, treat it as a source-specific caveat rather than a comparison blocker.
